title: Method for anchoring a conductive cap on a filled via in a printed circuit board and printed circuit board with an anchored conductive cap
abstract: The present invention relates to forming an anchored cap in a filled via in a PCB. The cap is formed by making an anchor opening in the filled via, depositing cooper into the anchor opening to form an anchor portion, and subsequently depositing a cap portion over the filled via and the anchor portion, so that the anchored cap is anchored to the filled via by the anchor portion. In oanother embodiment, the anchor porition and cap portion may be deposited in one step.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for forming an anchored cap on a filled via in a printed circuit boa (PCB), the method comprising: removing via fill material from the filled via to form an anchor opening; metalizing the anchor opening; forming an anchor portion in the filled via; and forming a conductive cap portion over the anchor portion to form an anchored c; 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the removing of the via fill material from the fills is performed using a plasma resin removal process. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the removing of the via fill material from the fills is performed by mechanical drilling. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the removing of the via fill material from the fills is performed by laser drilling. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the metalizing of the anchor opening is perfc using an electroless plating process. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the metalizing of the anchor opening is perfc using a sputtering process. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the metalizing of the anchor opening is perfc using a vapor deposition process. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the forming of the anchor portion in the fille comprises electroplating the metallized anchor opening and planarizing the printed circuit board. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the forming of the anchor portion in the filled i comprises: forming a first photoresist mask over the surface of the printed circuit board; electroplating the metallized anchor opening; removing the first photoresist mask; and planarizing the printed circuit board; and wherein the forming of the cap portion over the anchor portion comprises: forming a second photoresist mask over the surface of the printed circuit board; electroplating over the anchor portion; and removing the second photoresist mask. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the forming of the anchor portion in the filled concurrent with the forming of the conductive cap portion over the anchor portion. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the forming of the anchor portion in the filled vi the forming of the conductive cap portion over the anchor portion comprise: electroplating the metallized anchor opening and a region surrounding the a opening on the surface of the printed circuit board. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the forming of the anchor portion in the filled vi the forming of the conductive cap portion over the anchor portion comprise: forming a photoresist mask over the surface of the printed circuit board; electroplating the metallized anchor opening and a region surrounding the ai opening on the surface of the printed circuit board; and removing the photoresist mask. 13. An anchored cap on a filled via in a printed circuit board (PCB), the anchoret comprising: an anchor portion extending into an anchor opening in the filled via; and a conductive cap portion protruding above the surface of the PCB on top of the ai portion. 14. The anchored cap of claim 13, wherein the anchor portion in the filled via is 0.01 mils deep. 15. The anchored cap of claim 13, wherein the anchor portion in the filled via is 1 to 1C deep. 16. The anchored cap of claim 13, wherein the anchor opening has a diameter from 2 mils. 17. The anchored cap of claim 13, wherein the anchor opening has a diameter from 4 mils. 18. A printed circuit board (PCB) comprising: a substrate having a filled via, the filled via having an anchor opening; an anchor portion extending into the anchor opening in the filled via; and a conductive cap portion protruding above the surface of the PCB on top of anchor portion. 19. The PCB of claim 18, wherein the anchor portion in the filled via is 0.01 to 25 deep. 20. The PCB of claim 18, wherein the anchor portion in the filled via is 1 to 10 deep. 21. The PCB of claim 18, wherein the anchor opening has a diameter from 2 to 50 n 22. The PCB of claim 18, wherein the anchor opening has a diameter from 4 to 20 mils.
